scenarios.title: Meeting a New Classmate
scenarios.description: Practice saying hello and starting a conversation.

scenarios.title: Asking a Teacher for Help
scenarios.description: Practice how to ask your teacher for help politely.

scenarios.title: Joining a Group Project
scenarios.description: Learn how to join a group and contribute.

scenarios.title: Borrowing a Classmate's Pen
scenarios.description: Practice asking politely to borrow something.

scenarios.title: Apologizing to a Friend
scenarios.description: Learn to say sorry in a genuine and caring way.

scenarios.title: Saying No Politely
scenarios.description: Practice declining requests kindly but clearly.

scenarios.title: Giving a Compliment
scenarios.description: Practice noticing something good and saying it out loud.

scenarios.title: Accepting a Compliment
scenarios.description: Learn to receive kind words gracefully.

scenarios.title: Asking Someone to Repeat
scenarios.description: Practice saying you didn't hear and asking kindly.

scenarios.title: Sharing Your Opinion in Class
scenarios.description: Practice speaking up and sharing your view.

scenarios.title: Disagreeing Politely
scenarios.description: Share a different view without being rude.

scenarios.title: Accepting Feedback
scenarios.description: Learn to receive helpful feedback without getting defensive.

scenarios.title: Asking to Sit at Lunch
scenarios.description: Practice asking to join someone at the lunch table.

scenarios.title: Telling a Teacher About Late Work
scenarios.description: Practice owning up to late work honestly.

scenarios.title: Asking for More Time
scenarios.description: Practice requesting an extension politely.

scenarios.title: Expressing Frustration Calmly
scenarios.description: Learn to name and express frustration without losing control.

scenarios.title: Asking for a Break
scenarios.description: Practice recognising when you need a break and asking for one.

scenarios.title: Saying You Don't Understand
scenarios.description: Learn to ask for clarification when you are confused.

scenarios.title: Setting a Boundary Kindly
scenarios.description: Practice saying you are not comfortable with something.

scenarios.title: Asking for Some Space
scenarios.description: Practice asking kindly for time to yourself.

scenarios.title: Saying You Feel Overwhelmed
scenarios.description: Practice telling someone when you have too much going on.

scenarios.title: Talking About Feeling Left Out
scenarios.description: Practice speaking up when you feel excluded.

scenarios.title: Asking Someone to Forgive You
scenarios.description: Practice giving a genuine apology and asking for forgiveness.

scenarios.title: Forgiving a Friend
scenarios.description: Practice accepting an apology and choosing to move forward.

scenarios.title: Trying Something New
scenarios.description: Practice talking about nervousness and building courage to try new things.

scenarios.title: Buying Something at a Store
scenarios.description: Practice finding items, paying, and being polite with store staff.

scenarios.title: At the Doctor
scenarios.description: Practice checking in, describing symptoms, and understanding the treatment plan.

scenarios.title: Making a Phone Call
scenarios.description: Practice introducing yourself on a call, stating your purpose, and saying goodbye.

scenarios.title: At the Dentist
scenarios.description: Practice checking in, describing discomfort, and asking questions.

scenarios.title: Asking the Librarian for Help
scenarios.description: Practice asking for help finding a book, checking it out, and being polite.

scenarios.title: Taking the Bus
scenarios.description: Practice asking about routes, paying, and being polite on public transit.

scenarios.title: Picking Up Medicine
scenarios.description: Practice giving your name, asking questions, and understanding instructions.

scenarios.title: Returning Something to a Store
scenarios.description: Practice politely explaining a return, providing proof, and resolving the issue.

scenarios.title: Asking for Directions
scenarios.description: Practice politely asking where something is and understanding the response.

scenarios.title: Getting a Haircut
scenarios.description: Practice describing what you want at the barber or hair salon.

scenarios.title: Picking Up a Food Order
scenarios.description: Practice giving your name, confirming your order, and handling issues politely.

scenarios.title: Asking a Neighbor for Help
scenarios.description: Practice introducing yourself and making a polite request to a neighbor.

scenarios.title: Talking to Your Coach
scenarios.description: Practice asking about practice, accepting feedback, and communicating your needs.

scenarios.title: Ordering at a Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Practice ordering a drink, customizing it, and handling payment politely.

scenarios.title: Introduction at Job Shadowing
scenarios.description: Practice professional greetings, small talk, and showing interest in a workplace.

scenarios.title: Planning to Hang Out
scenarios.description: Practice suggesting an activity, agreeing on a time, and confirming plans with a friend.

scenarios.title: Talking About a Hobby
scenarios.description: Practice sharing what you enjoy and showing interest in someone else's hobbies.

scenarios.title: Clearing Up a Misunderstanding
scenarios.description: Practice calmly explaining yourself and listening when someone has the wrong idea.

scenarios.title: Comforting an Upset Friend
scenarios.description: Practice noticing when a friend is sad and knowing what to say to help.

scenarios.title: Inviting Someone to an Event
scenarios.description: Practice making a clear and friendly invitation and handling yes or no gracefully.

scenarios.title: Responding to Teasing Calmly
scenarios.description: Practice staying calm, using assertive language, and knowing when to walk away.

scenarios.title: Calling a Friend
scenarios.description: Practice starting a phone call, chatting naturally, and saying a proper goodbye.

scenarios.title: Small Talk at a Party
scenarios.description: Practice starting conversations with people you do not know well at a social event.

scenarios.title: Congratulating Someone
scenarios.description: Practice giving genuine praise and celebrating someone else is success warmly.

scenarios.title: Saying a Meaningful Thank You
scenarios.description: Practice expressing sincere gratitude that goes beyond just saying the words.

gr_a1_bridge.say: You see someone new sitting alone in class. Time to say hello.

gr_a1_q1.say: Can you wave and say hi to the new student?

gr_a1_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Can you wave and say hi to the new student?

gr_a1_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hi there! I'm new here too. Let's keep going.

gr_a1_q2.say: They smile back. Can you tell them your name?
gr_a1_q2.hint: Say: My name is ... or I'm ...

gr_a1_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They smile back. Can you tell them your name?
gr_a1_q2_r.hint: Say: My name is ... or I'm ...

gr_a1_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: My name is Alex. Nice to meet you. Let's keep going.

gr_a1_q3.say: They say their name is Sam. Can you say it's nice to meet them?

gr_a1_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say their name is Sam. Can you say it's nice to meet them?

gr_a1_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Nice to meet you, Sam. Let's keep going.

gr_a1_q4.say: Sam asks which class you are in. Can you tell them?
gr_a1_q4.hint: Say: I'm in grade ... or I'm in room ...

gr_a1_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam asks which class you are in. Can you tell them?
gr_a1_q4_r.hint: Say: I'm in grade ... or I'm in room ...

gr_a1_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I'm in grade ten, room twelve. Let's keep going.

gr_a1_q5.say: Sam seems friendly. Can you say something positive like this seems like a good class?
gr_a1_q5.hint: Say: This seems like a fun class!

gr_a1_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam seems friendly. Can you say something positive like this seems like a good class?
gr_a1_q5_r.hint: Say: This seems like a fun class!

gr_a1_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: This looks like a really good class. Let's keep going.

gr_a2_bridge.say: You and Sam are getting along. Let's keep the conversation going.

gr_a2_q1.say: Ask Sam if they just moved to this school.
gr_a2_q1.hint: Say: Did you just move here? or Are you new?

gr_a2_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask Sam if they just moved to this school.
gr_a2_q1_r.hint: Say: Did you just move here? or Are you new?

gr_a2_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Did you just move here? I noticed I haven't seen you before. Let's keep going.

gr_a2_q2.say: Sam says yes they moved last week. Say something kind about that.
gr_a2_q2.hint: Say: That must be exciting or That's a big change!

gr_a2_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam says yes they moved last week. Say something kind about that.
gr_a2_q2_r.hint: Say: That must be exciting or That's a big change!

gr_a2_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: That must be exciting but also a big change. Let's keep going.

gr_a2_q3.say: Tell Sam you remember what it felt like to be new.
gr_a2_q3.hint: Say: I know how that feels or I remember being new too

gr_a2_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell Sam you remember what it felt like to be new.
gr_a2_q3_r.hint: Say: I know how that feels or I remember being new too

gr_a2_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I know how that feels. I remember being new here. Let's keep going.

gr_a2_q4.say: Sam looks relieved. Can you tell them the teachers here are nice?
gr_a2_q4.hint: Say: The teachers here are really nice and helpful

gr_a2_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam looks relieved. Can you tell them the teachers here are nice?
gr_a2_q4_r.hint: Say: The teachers here are really nice and helpful

gr_a2_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: The teachers here are really nice and helpful. Let's keep going.

gr_a2_q5.say: Offer to show Sam around the school.
gr_a2_q5.hint: Say: I can show you around if you want

gr_a2_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Offer to show Sam around the school.
gr_a2_q5_r.hint: Say: I can show you around if you want

gr_a2_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I can show you around the school if you'd like. Let's keep going.

gr_a3_bridge.say: Now let's find out a bit more about Sam.

gr_a3_q1.say: Ask Sam what their favourite subject is.
gr_a3_q1.hint: Say: What's your favourite subject? or What class do you like?

gr_a3_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask Sam what their favourite subject is.
gr_a3_q1_r.hint: Say: What's your favourite subject? or What class do you like?

gr_a3_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: What is your favourite subject?. Let's keep going.

gr_a3_q2.say: Sam says they love science. React positively.
gr_a3_q2.hint: Say: That's cool! Science is great or I like science too

gr_a3_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam says they love science. React positively.
gr_a3_q2_r.hint: Say: That's cool! Science is great or I like science too

gr_a3_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: That's cool! Science is one of my favourite subjects too. Let's keep going.

gr_a3_q3.say: Tell Sam what your favourite subject is.
gr_a3_q3.hint: Say: Mine is ... or I really enjoy ...

gr_a3_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell Sam what your favourite subject is.
gr_a3_q3_r.hint: Say: Mine is ... or I really enjoy ...

gr_a3_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: My favourite is English. I love reading and writing. Let's keep going.

gr_a3_q4.say: Ask Sam if they play any sports or have hobbies.
gr_a3_q4.hint: Say: Do you play any sports? or What do you like to do?

gr_a3_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask Sam if they play any sports or have hobbies.
gr_a3_q4_r.hint: Say: Do you play any sports? or What do you like to do?

gr_a3_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Do you play any sports or have any hobbies?. Let's keep going.

gr_a3_q5.say: Sam mentions they play basketball. Show interest.
gr_a3_q5.hint: Say: That's awesome! I love watching basketball

gr_a3_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam mentions they play basketball. Show interest.
gr_a3_q5_r.hint: Say: That's awesome! I love watching basketball

gr_a3_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: That's awesome! Basketball is so much fun to watch. Let's keep going.

gr_a4_bridge.say: You're doing great. Let's talk about things you have in common.

gr_a4_q1.say: Ask Sam if they watch any TV shows or listen to music.
gr_a4_q1.hint: Say: Do you watch any good shows? or What music do you like?

gr_a4_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask Sam if they watch any TV shows or listen to music.
gr_a4_q1_r.hint: Say: Do you watch any good shows? or What music do you like?

gr_a4_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Do you watch any good TV shows or listen to music?. Let's keep going.

gr_a4_q2.say: Sam names a show you've seen. Tell them you've seen it.
gr_a4_q2.hint: Say: I've seen that! or I know that show, it's great!

gr_a4_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam names a show you've seen. Tell them you've seen it.
gr_a4_q2_r.hint: Say: I've seen that! or I know that show, it's great!

gr_a4_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I've seen that show! It's really good. Let's keep going.

gr_a4_q3.say: Share your favourite show or song with Sam.
gr_a4_q3.hint: Say: My favourite show is ... or I really like ...

gr_a4_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Share your favourite show or song with Sam.
gr_a4_q3_r.hint: Say: My favourite show is ... or I really like ...

gr_a4_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: My favourite show is one about cooking competitions. Let's keep going.

gr_a4_q4.say: Sam smiles. Can you say you think you two will get along well?
gr_a4_q4.hint: Say: I think we're going to get along really well

gr_a4_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam smiles. Can you say you think you two will get along well?
gr_a4_q4_r.hint: Say: I think we're going to get along really well

gr_a4_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I think we are going to get along really well. Let's keep going.

gr_a4_q5.say: Ask Sam if they want to sit together at lunch.
gr_a4_q5.hint: Say: Do you want to sit together at lunch?

gr_a4_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask Sam if they want to sit together at lunch.
gr_a4_q5_r.hint: Say: Do you want to sit together at lunch?

gr_a4_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Do you want to sit together at lunch today?. Let's keep going.

gr_a5_bridge.say: Almost done — let's wrap this conversation up nicely.

gr_a5_q1.say: Sam says yes to lunch! Show you're happy about that.
gr_a5_q1.hint: Say: Great! or That's awesome! I'm really glad

gr_a5_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam says yes to lunch! Show you're happy about that.
gr_a5_q1_r.hint: Say: Great! or That's awesome! I'm really glad

gr_a5_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Great! I'm really glad. It will be fun. Let's keep going.

gr_a5_q2.say: Tell Sam you can introduce them to your other friends.
gr_a5_q2.hint: Say: I can introduce you to my friends too

gr_a5_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell Sam you can introduce them to your other friends.
gr_a5_q2_r.hint: Say: I can introduce you to my friends too

gr_a5_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I can also introduce you to some of my other friends. Let's keep going.

gr_a5_q3.say: Sam thanks you for being so welcoming. Respond kindly.
gr_a5_q3.hint: Say: Of course! or No problem, I'm happy to help

gr_a5_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam thanks you for being so welcoming. Respond kindly.
gr_a5_q3_r.hint: Say: Of course! or No problem, I'm happy to help

gr_a5_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Of course! No problem at all. I'm happy to help. Let's keep going.

gr_a5_q4.say: Ask Sam if they are feeling less nervous about the new school.
gr_a5_q4.hint: Say: Are you feeling a bit better about everything?

gr_a5_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask Sam if they are feeling less nervous about the new school.
gr_a5_q4_r.hint: Say: Are you feeling a bit better about everything?

gr_a5_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Are you feeling a bit less nervous about everything?. Let's keep going.

gr_a5_q5.say: Sam says yes. Say something encouraging to close.
gr_a5_q5.hint: Say: Good! You've got this. And I'm here if you need anything

gr_a5_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam says yes. Say something encouraging to close.
gr_a5_q5_r.hint: Say: Good! You've got this. And I'm here if you need anything

gr_a5_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Good! You've got this. And I'm here if you need anything. Let's keep going.

gr_a6_bridge.say: The school day is almost over. One last friendly exchange.

gr_a6_q1.say: Tell Sam it was great meeting them today.
gr_a6_q1.hint: Say: It was really great meeting you today!

gr_a6_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell Sam it was great meeting them today.
gr_a6_q1_r.hint: Say: It was really great meeting you today!

gr_a6_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: It was really great meeting you today. Let's keep going.

gr_a6_q2.say: Ask Sam if they found their way around okay.
gr_a6_q2.hint: Say: Did you find your way around okay? or Did you get lost?

gr_a6_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask Sam if they found their way around okay.
gr_a6_q2_r.hint: Say: Did you find your way around okay? or Did you get lost?

gr_a6_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Did you manage to find your way around okay?. Let's keep going.

gr_a6_q3.say: Sam laughs and says a little bit. Sympathize and laugh.
gr_a6_q3.hint: Say: Ha! I got lost on my first day too, don't worry

gr_a6_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam laughs and says a little bit. Sympathize and laugh.
gr_a6_q3_r.hint: Say: Ha! I got lost on my first day too, don't worry

gr_a6_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Ha! I got lost on my first day too. It happens. Let's keep going.

gr_a6_q4.say: Remind Sam they can always ask you for help.
gr_a6_q4.hint: Say: You can always ask me if you need help

gr_a6_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Remind Sam they can always ask you for help.
gr_a6_q4_r.hint: Say: You can always ask me if you need help

gr_a6_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: You can always ask me if you need help with anything. Let's keep going.

gr_a6_q5.say: Sam smiles. Give them one final encouraging word.
gr_a6_q5.hint: Say: You're going to be just fine here. See you tomorrow!

gr_a6_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam smiles. Give them one final encouraging word.
gr_a6_q5_r.hint: Say: You're going to be just fine here. See you tomorrow!

gr_a6_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: You are going to be just fine here. See you tomorrow. Let's keep going.

gr_a7_bridge.say: Next day — you see Sam again in the hallway.

gr_a7_q1.say: Wave to Sam in the hallway and say good morning.
gr_a7_q1.hint: Say: Good morning! or Hey Sam!

gr_a7_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Wave to Sam in the hallway and say good morning.
gr_a7_q1_r.hint: Say: Good morning! or Hey Sam!

gr_a7_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Good morning Sam! Great to see you again. Let's keep going.

gr_a7_q2.say: Ask Sam how their evening was.
gr_a7_q2.hint: Say: How was your evening? or Did you have a good night?

gr_a7_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask Sam how their evening was.
gr_a7_q2_r.hint: Say: How was your evening? or Did you have a good night?

gr_a7_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: How was your evening? Did you settle in okay?. Let's keep going.

gr_a7_q3.say: Sam says it was good. Say that's great to hear.
gr_a7_q3.hint: Say: That's great to hear! or Glad to hear that

gr_a7_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam says it was good. Say that's great to hear.
gr_a7_q3_r.hint: Say: That's great to hear! or Glad to hear that

gr_a7_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: That is great to hear! I am glad. Let's keep going.

gr_a7_q4.say: Tell Sam you saved them a seat in class.
gr_a7_q4.hint: Say: I saved you a seat in class!

gr_a7_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell Sam you saved them a seat in class.
gr_a7_q4_r.hint: Say: I saved you a seat in class!

gr_a7_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I saved you a seat in class. Come sit with me. Let's keep going.

gr_a7_q5.say: Sam beams. Invite them to walk to class together.
gr_a7_q5.hint: Say: Let's walk to class together!

gr_a7_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sam beams. Invite them to walk to class together.
gr_a7_q5_r.hint: Say: Let's walk to class together!

gr_a7_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Let's walk to class together. Come on. Let's keep going.

gr_a8_bridge.say: Final act — introducing Sam to another friend.

gr_a8_q1.say: You spot your friend Jamie. Tell Sam you want to introduce them.
gr_a8_q1.hint: Say: I want you to meet my friend!

gr_a8_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You spot your friend Jamie. Tell Sam you want to introduce them.
gr_a8_q1_r.hint: Say: I want you to meet my friend!

gr_a8_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I want you to meet one of my friends. Let's keep going.

gr_a8_q2.say: Walk up to Jamie and say hey, you want them to meet someone.
gr_a8_q2.hint: Say: Hey Jamie! I want you to meet Sam

gr_a8_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Walk up to Jamie and say hey, you want them to meet someone.
gr_a8_q2_r.hint: Say: Hey Jamie! I want you to meet Sam

gr_a8_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hey Jamie! I want you to meet Sam, they're new here. Let's keep going.

gr_a8_q3.say: Tell Jamie something nice about Sam.
gr_a8_q3.hint: Say: Sam is really nice. We have a lot in common

gr_a8_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell Jamie something nice about Sam.
gr_a8_q3_r.hint: Say: Sam is really nice. We have a lot in common

gr_a8_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Sam is really nice. We have a lot in common. Let's keep going.

gr_a8_q4.say: Encourage Sam to say hello to Jamie.
gr_a8_q4.hint: Say: Go ahead Sam, say hi!

gr_a8_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Encourage Sam to say hello to Jamie.
gr_a8_q4_r.hint: Say: Go ahead Sam, say hi!

gr_a8_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Go ahead Sam, say hi! Jamie is really friendly. Let's keep going.

gr_a8_q5.say: All three of you laugh. Say this is the start of a great friendship.
gr_a8_q5.hint: Say: I think this is the start of a great friendship!

gr_a8_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. All three of you laugh. Say this is the start of a great friendship.
gr_a8_q5_r.hint: Say: I think this is the start of a great friendship!

gr_a8_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I think this is the start of a great friendship. Let's keep going.

gr_win.say: Amazing! You made a new friend today. That took courage. You earned a star!

at_a1_bridge.say: You are in class and there is something you do not understand.

at_a1_q1.say: The teacher is at their desk. How do you get their attention politely?
at_a1_q1.hint: Say: Excuse me or Raise your hand

at_a1_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The teacher is at their desk. How do you get their attention politely?
at_a1_q1_r.hint: Say: Excuse me or Raise your hand

at_a1_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me, could I ask you something?. Let's keep going.

at_a1_q2.say: The teacher looks up and says yes. How do you say you need help?
at_a1_q2.hint: Say: I need some help please or I have a question

at_a1_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The teacher looks up and says yes. How do you say you need help?
at_a1_q2_r.hint: Say: I need some help please or I have a question

at_a1_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I need some help please. I have a question. Let's keep going.

at_a1_q3.say: The teacher nods. Tell them which subject or topic this is about.
at_a1_q3.hint: Say: It's about ... or I'm working on ...

at_a1_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The teacher nods. Tell them which subject or topic this is about.
at_a1_q3_r.hint: Say: It's about ... or I'm working on ...

at_a1_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: It is about the maths problem we were just doing. Let's keep going.

at_a1_q4.say: Ask if this is a good time to talk.
at_a1_q4.hint: Say: Is this a good time? or Do you have a minute?

at_a1_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask if this is a good time to talk.
at_a1_q4_r.hint: Say: Is this a good time? or Do you have a minute?

at_a1_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Is this a good time to ask, or should I come back?. Let's keep going.

at_a3_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell the teacher you will try the problem on your own.

at_win.say: Well done! You asked for help clearly and politely. That is a great skill. You earned a star!

gp_a1_bridge.say: You see a group of classmates working on a project nearby.

gp_a1_q1.say: Walk towards the group. Can you say excuse me to get their attention?
gp_a1_q1.hint: Say: Excuse me or Hey, could I talk to you?

gp_a1_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Walk towards the group. Can you say excuse me to get their attention?
gp_a1_q1_r.hint: Say: Excuse me or Hey, could I talk to you?

gp_a1_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me, I don't mean to interrupt. Let's keep going.

gp_a1_q2.say: They look up. Ask if they are working on the class project.
gp_a1_q2.hint: Say: Are you working on the class project?

gp_a1_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They look up. Ask if they are working on the class project.
gp_a1_q2_r.hint: Say: Are you working on the class project?

gp_a1_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Are you all working on the class project?. Let's keep going.

gp_a1_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say yes. Ask if you could possibly join their group.
gp_a1_q3_r.hint: Say: Could I join your group? or Is there room for me?

gp_a1_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Could I possibly join your group? I need a group too. Let's keep going.

gp_a1_q4.hint: Say: Thank you so much! I am really happy to join

gp_a1_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They agree. Say thank you and that you are happy to join.
gp_a1_q4_r.hint: Say: Thank you so much! I am really happy to join

gp_a1_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you! I am really happy to be part of the group. Let's keep going.

gp_a2_bridge.say: Now let's find out what the project is about.

gp_a2_q1_r.hint: Say: What is the project about?

gp_a2_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: What is the project topic? I just want to catch up. Let's keep going.

gp_a2_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: That sounds really interesting! I like that topic. Let's keep going.

gp_a2_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask how far along they are.
gp_a2_q3_r.hint: Say: How far along are you? or How much have you done?

gp_a2_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: How far along are you all? What have you covered so far?. Let's keep going.

gp_a2_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They explain what they have done. Compliment their work.
gp_a2_q4_r.hint: Say: Wow you have done a lot already! That is great

gp_a2_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Wow you have done quite a lot already. That is really good. Let's keep going.

gp_a2_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask if there is a part you could help with.
gp_a2_q5_r.hint: Say: Is there a part I can help with?

gp_a3_q3_r.hint: Say: Happy to take that on! or Sure, I can do that

ss_a6_q5_r.hint: Say: I want to be the kind of friend you can always count on

sn_a3_q2.hint: Say: I still want to hang out — just not like this

sn_a3_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say you still want to spend time with them — just not this way.
sn_a3_q2_r.hint: Say: I still want to hang out — just not like this

sn_a3_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I still want to hang out with you. Just not in this way. Let's keep going.

sn_a3_q3.say: Suggest an alternative you are both comfortable with.
sn_a3_q3.hint: Say: How about we go to the cafe instead?

sn_a3_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Suggest an alternative you are both comfortable with.
sn_a3_q3_r.hint: Say: How about we go to the cafe instead?

sn_a3_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: How about we go get lunch at the cafe instead? My treat. Let's keep going.

sn_a3_q4.say: They agree. Say you are glad you worked it out.
sn_a3_q4.hint: Say: I am glad we worked that out!

sn_a3_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They agree. Say you are glad you worked it out.
sn_a3_q4_r.hint: Say: I am glad we worked that out!

sn_a3_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am glad we could work it out together. That is what friends do. Let's keep going.

sn_a3_q5.say: Tell them you appreciate that they did not pressure you.
sn_a3_q5.hint: Say: I appreciate that you didn't pressure me

sn_a3_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them you appreciate that they did not pressure you.
sn_a3_q5_r.hint: Say: I appreciate that you didn't pressure me

sn_a3_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I really appreciate that you didn't keep pressuring me about it. Let's keep going.

sn_a4_bridge.say: Practising saying no in a different situation.

sn_a4_q1.say: Someone asks you to share your homework answers. Say no kindly.
sn_a4_q1.hint: Say: Sorry I can't share my answers

sn_a4_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Someone asks you to share your homework answers. Say no kindly.
sn_a4_q1_r.hint: Say: Sorry I can't share my answers

sn_a4_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Sorry, I can't share my answers. That would not be right. Let's keep going.

sn_a4_q2.say: Offer to help them understand the work instead.
sn_a4_q2.hint: Say: I can help you understand the work instead

sn_a4_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Offer to help them understand the work instead.
sn_a4_q2_r.hint: Say: I can help you understand the work instead

sn_a4_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I can help you understand how to do it yourself instead. Let's keep going.

sn_a4_q3.say: They agree. Feel good about your decision.
sn_a4_q3.hint: Say: I feel good about that decision

sn_a4_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They agree. Feel good about your decision.
sn_a4_q3_r.hint: Say: I feel good about that decision

sn_a4_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I feel really good about that decision. It was the right choice. Let's keep going.

sn_a4_q4.say: Remind yourself that saying no is okay and healthy.
sn_a4_q4.hint: Say: Saying no is okay and healthy

sn_a4_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Remind yourself that saying no is okay and healthy.
sn_a4_q4_r.hint: Say: Saying no is okay and healthy

sn_a4_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Saying no when something is wrong is okay and important. Let's keep going.

sn_a4_q5.say: Practice saying no clearly in a calm voice.
sn_a4_q5.hint: Say: No thank you or I don't think that is right for me

sn_a4_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Practice saying no clearly in a calm voice.
sn_a4_q5_r.hint: Say: No thank you or I don't think that is right for me

sn_a4_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: No thank you. That is not something I want to do. Let's keep going.

sn_a5_bridge.say: Reflecting on why saying no matters.

sn_a5_q1.say: Think about why it is important to say no sometimes.
sn_a5_q1.hint: Say: It is important to know when to say no

sn_a5_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Think about why it is important to say no sometimes.
sn_a5_q1_r.hint: Say: It is important to know when to say no

sn_a5_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: It is really important to know when and how to say no. Let's keep going.

sn_a5_q2.say: Tell a trusted friend you practiced saying no today.
sn_a5_q2.hint: Say: I practiced saying no today and it felt good

sn_a5_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell a trusted friend you practiced saying no today.
sn_a5_q2_r.hint: Say: I practiced saying no today and it felt good

sn_a5_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I practiced saying no today and it actually felt really good. Let's keep going.

sn_a5_q3.say: They ask how it went. Tell them it went well.
sn_a5_q3.hint: Say: It went really well! Better than I expected

sn_a5_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask how it went. Tell them it went well.
sn_a5_q3_r.hint: Say: It went really well! Better than I expected

sn_a5_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: It went really well! Better than I expected actually. Let's keep going.

sn_a5_q4.say: Say you used to find it hard to say no but not anymore.
sn_a5_q4.hint: Say: I used to find it really hard but it is easier now

sn_a5_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say you used to find it hard to say no but not anymore.
sn_a5_q4_r.hint: Say: I used to find it really hard but it is easier now

sn_a5_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I used to find it so hard to say no but it gets easier. Let's keep going.

sn_a5_q5.say: Encourage your friend to practice too.
sn_a5_q5.hint: Say: You should practice too! It is really worth it

sn_a5_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Encourage your friend to practice too.
sn_a5_q5_r.hint: Say: You should practice too! It is really worth it

sn_a5_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: You should practice saying no too. It is really worth it. Let's keep going.

sn_a6_bridge.say: Being assertive in a new situation.

sn_a6_q1.say: Someone pushes in front of you in a queue. Speak up.
sn_a6_q1.hint: Say: Excuse me, I was here first

sn_a6_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Someone pushes in front of you in a queue. Speak up.
sn_a6_q1_r.hint: Say: Excuse me, I was here first

sn_a6_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me, I think I was here first actually. Let's keep going.

sn_a6_q2.hint: Say: No problem, thank you for understanding

sn_a6_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They apologise. Accept it graciously.
sn_a6_q2_r.hint: Say: No problem, thank you for understanding

sn_a6_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: No problem at all. Thank you for understanding. Let's keep going.

sn_a6_q3.say: Tell yourself you handled that well.
sn_a6_q3.hint: Say: I handled that really well!

sn_a6_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell yourself you handled that well.
sn_a6_q3_r.hint: Say: I handled that really well!

sn_a6_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I handled that really well. I am proud of myself. Let's keep going.

sn_a6_q4.say: Think about how standing up for yourself feels good.
sn_a6_q4.hint: Say: Standing up for myself feels really good

sn_a6_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Think about how standing up for yourself feels good.
sn_a6_q4_r.hint: Say: Standing up for myself feels really good

sn_a6_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Standing up for myself in a calm way feels really good. Let's keep going.

sn_a6_q5.say: Remind yourself to always be respectful even when assertive.
sn_a6_q5.hint: Say: I can be assertive and still be respectful

sn_a6_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Remind yourself to always be respectful even when assertive.
sn_a6_q5_r.hint: Say: I can be assertive and still be respectful

sn_a6_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I can be assertive while still being calm and respectful. Let's keep going.

sn_a7_bridge.say: Final act — a full practice of saying no confidently.

sn_a7_q1.say: Someone asks you to do something that doesn't feel right.
sn_a7_q1.hint: Say: No, that doesn't feel right to me

sn_a7_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Someone asks you to do something that doesn't feel right.
sn_a7_q1_r.hint: Say: No, that doesn't feel right to me

sn_a7_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: No, that doesn't feel right to me and I don't want to do it. Let's keep going.

sn_a7_q2.say: Give your reason briefly and clearly.
sn_a7_q2.hint: Say: Because I don't want to and it is not right

sn_a7_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Give your reason briefly and clearly.
sn_a7_q2_r.hint: Say: Because I don't want to and it is not right

sn_a7_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Because it is not something I want to do. It doesn't feel right. Let's keep going.

sn_a7_q3.say: Offer an alternative if there is one.
sn_a7_q3.hint: Say: How about we do something else instead?

sn_a7_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Offer an alternative if there is one.
sn_a7_q3_r.hint: Say: How about we do something else instead?

sn_a7_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: How about we do something completely different instead?. Let's keep going.

sn_a7_q4.say: Stay calm and do not apologise for saying no.
sn_a7_q4.hint: Say: I do not need to apologise for saying no

sn_a7_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Stay calm and do not apologise for saying no.
sn_a7_q4_r.hint: Say: I do not need to apologise for saying no

sn_a7_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am not apologising for saying no. It is my right to say no. Let's keep going.

sn_a7_q5.say: Feel confident and proud of saying no when needed.
sn_a7_q5.hint: Say: I am proud that I said no when I needed to

sn_a7_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Feel confident and proud of saying no when needed.
sn_a7_q5_r.hint: Say: I am proud that I said no when I needed to

sn_a7_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am really proud of myself for saying no when it was needed. Let's keep going.

sn_win.say: Excellent! You said no politely and held your ground. That is a very important skill. You earned a star!

gc_a1_bridge.say: You want to say something nice to a classmate.

gc_a1_q1.say: Notice something positive about your classmate.
gc_a1_q1.hint: Say: I notice you did really well or I like your...

gc_a1_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Notice something positive about your classmate.
gc_a1_q1_r.hint: Say: I notice you did really well or I like your...

gc_a1_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I noticed something really nice about you today. Let's keep going.

gc_a1_q2.say: Think about how to say it in a kind way.
gc_a1_q2.hint: Say: Let me think of a kind way to say this

gc_a1_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Think about how to say it in a kind way.
gc_a1_q2_r.hint: Say: Let me think of a kind way to say this

gc_a1_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Let me think about the best and kindest way to say this. Let's keep going.

gc_a1_q3.hint: Say: Excuse me or Hey, can I say something?

gc_a1_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Get their attention politely.
gc_a1_q3_r.hint: Say: Excuse me or Hey, can I say something?

gc_a1_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me, can I say something to you quickly?. Let's keep going.

gc_a1_q4.say: Start with something like hey I just wanted to say...
gc_a1_q4.hint: Say: Hey, I just wanted to say something nice

gc_a1_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Start with something like hey I just wanted to say...
gc_a1_q4_r.hint: Say: Hey, I just wanted to say something nice

gc_a1_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hey, I just wanted to say something nice to you if that is okay. Let's keep going.

gc_a1_q5.say: Say the compliment clearly and sincerely.
gc_a1_q5.hint: Say: You did a really great job! or I love your...

gc_a1_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say the compliment clearly and sincerely.
gc_a1_q5_r.hint: Say: You did a really great job! or I love your...

gc_a1_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: You did such a great job with your presentation today. Let's keep going.

gc_a2_q1.say: Tell them exactly what you liked or admired.
gc_a2_q1.hint: Say: What I really liked was... or The part I loved was...

gc_a2_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them exactly what you liked or admired.
gc_a2_q1_r.hint: Say: What I really liked was... or The part I loved was...

gc_a2_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: What I really liked was how clearly you explained everything. Let's keep going.

gc_a2_q2.say: Say why that thing stood out to you.
gc_a2_q2.hint: Say: It stood out to me because...

gc_a2_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say why that thing stood out to you.
gc_a2_q2_r.hint: Say: It stood out to me because...

gc_a2_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: It really stood out to me because you made it so easy to understand. Let's keep going.

gc_a2_q3.say: Tell them it inspired or motivated you.
gc_a2_q3.hint: Say: It inspired me to try harder too

gc_a2_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them it inspired or motivated you.
gc_a2_q3_r.hint: Say: It inspired me to try harder too

gc_a2_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: It actually inspired me to want to work harder on my own work. Let's keep going.

gc_a2_q4.say: Ask them how they got so good at that.
gc_a2_q4.hint: Say: How did you get so good at that?

gc_a2_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask them how they got so good at that.
gc_a2_q4_r.hint: Say: How did you get so good at that?

gc_a2_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: How did you get so good at presenting? Do you practice a lot?. Let's keep going.

gc_a2_q5.say: Listen to their answer and show genuine interest.
gc_a2_q5.hint: Say: That is really interesting! I didn't know that

gc_a2_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Listen to their answer and show genuine interest.
gc_a2_q5_r.hint: Say: That is really interesting! I didn't know that

gc_a2_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Oh that is so interesting! I did not know that about you. Let's keep going.

gc_a3_bridge.say: Responding when they react to your compliment.

gc_a3_q1.say: They say thank you. Tell them you really meant it.
gc_a3_q1.hint: Say: I really meant it! It was genuine

gc_a3_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say thank you. Tell them you really meant it.
gc_a3_q1_r.hint: Say: I really meant it! It was genuine

gc_a3_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I really meant it. It was a completely genuine compliment. Let's keep going.

gc_a3_q2.say: Say it makes you happy to tell people nice things.
gc_a3_q2.hint: Say: It makes me happy to tell people nice things

gc_a3_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say it makes you happy to tell people nice things.
gc_a3_q2_r.hint: Say: It makes me happy to tell people nice things

gc_a3_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: It genuinely makes me happy to tell people nice things about themselves. Let's keep going.

gc_a3_q3.say: Ask them if anyone else has complimented them on that today.
gc_a3_q3.hint: Say: Has anyone else told you that today?

gc_a3_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask them if anyone else has complimented them on that today.
gc_a3_q3_r.hint: Say: Has anyone else told you that today?

gc_a3_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Has anyone else told you that today? Because they should have!. Let's keep going.

gc_a3_q4.say: They smile shyly. Tell them they should be proud.
gc_a3_q4.hint: Say: You should be really proud of yourself!

gc_a3_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They smile shyly. Tell them they should be proud.
gc_a3_q4_r.hint: Say: You should be really proud of yourself!

gc_a3_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: You should be really proud of yourself. You worked hard for that. Let's keep going.

gc_a3_q5.say: End the exchange with a smile and something warm.
gc_a3_q5.hint: Say: Keep it up! You are doing amazing

gc_a3_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. End the exchange with a smile and something warm.
gc_a3_q5_r.hint: Say: Keep it up! You are doing amazing

gc_a3_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Keep it up! You are truly doing amazing work. Let's keep going.

gc_a4_bridge.say: Giving a compliment to someone you don't know well.

gc_a4_q1.say: You notice a classmate you don't know well did something great.
gc_a4_q1.hint: Say: I notice that person did something great

gc_a4_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You notice a classmate you don't know well did something great.
gc_a4_q1_r.hint: Say: I notice that person did something great

gc_a4_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I noticed that person I don't know very well did something amazing. Let's keep going.

gc_a4_q2.say: Walk over and introduce yourself first.
gc_a4_q2.hint: Say: Hi, I don't think we have met properly. I'm...

gc_a4_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Walk over and introduce yourself first.
gc_a4_q2_r.hint: Say: Hi, I don't think we have met properly. I'm...

gc_a4_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hi! I don't think we have properly met. My name is Alex. Let's keep going.

gc_a4_q3.say: After the introduction, give the compliment.
gc_a4_q3.hint: Say: I just wanted to say that was really impressive

gc_a4_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. After the introduction, give the compliment.
gc_a4_q3_r.hint: Say: I just wanted to say that was really impressive

gc_a4_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I just wanted to say that what you did there was really impressive. Let's keep going.

gc_a4_q4.say: They look surprised but pleased. Tell them you noticed their hard work.
gc_a4_q4.hint: Say: I noticed how much hard work you put in

gc_a4_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They look surprised but pleased. Tell them you noticed their hard work.
gc_a4_q4_r.hint: Say: I noticed how much hard work you put in

gc_a4_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I noticed how much hard work and effort you put into that. Let's keep going.

gc_a4_q5.say: Say you hope to see more of that work in the future.
gc_a4_q5.hint: Say: I hope to see more great work from you!

gc_a4_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say you hope to see more of that work in the future.
gc_a4_q5_r.hint: Say: I hope to see more great work from you!

gc_a4_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I really hope to see more great work from you in the future. Let's keep going.

gc_a5_q1.say: Tell a friend about the compliment you gave today.
gc_a5_q1.hint: Say: I gave someone a compliment today

gc_a5_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell a friend about the compliment you gave today.
gc_a5_q1_r.hint: Say: I gave someone a compliment today

gc_a5_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I gave someone a really genuine compliment today and it felt great. Let's keep going.

gc_a5_q2.say: Say how it made both of you feel.
gc_a5_q2.hint: Say: It made us both feel really good!

gc_a5_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say how it made both of you feel.
gc_a5_q2_r.hint: Say: It made us both feel really good!

gc_a5_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: It made both of us feel really good. That was nice. Let's keep going.

gc_a5_q3.say: Encourage your friend to try giving a compliment too.
gc_a5_q3.hint: Say: You should try giving a compliment too!

gc_a5_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Encourage your friend to try giving a compliment too.
gc_a5_q3_r.hint: Say: You should try giving a compliment too!

gc_a5_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: You should try it too. Giving compliments feels really great. Let's keep going.

gc_a5_q4.say: Say that compliments cost nothing but mean everything.
gc_a5_q4.hint: Say: Compliments cost nothing but mean everything

gc_a5_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say that compliments cost nothing but mean everything.
gc_a5_q4_r.hint: Say: Compliments cost nothing but mean everything

gc_a5_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Compliments cost nothing but they can mean everything to someone. Let's keep going.

gc_a5_q5.say: Decide to make it a habit to give one compliment a day.
gc_a5_q5.hint: Say: I am going to try to give one compliment a day

gc_a5_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Decide to make it a habit to give one compliment a day.
gc_a5_q5_r.hint: Say: I am going to try to give one compliment a day

gc_a5_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am going to try to give at least one genuine compliment every day. Let's keep going.

gc_a6_bridge.say: Complimenting someone on their personality, not just their work.

gc_a6_q1.say: Think of a kind thing to say about someone's personality.
gc_a6_q1.hint: Say: I want to compliment their personality

gc_a6_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Think of a kind thing to say about someone's personality.
gc_a6_q1_r.hint: Say: I want to compliment their personality

gc_a6_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I want to give a compliment about who they are, not just what they do. Let's keep going.

gc_a6_q2.say: Tell them they are one of the kindest people you know.
gc_a6_q2.hint: Say: You are one of the kindest people I know

gc_a6_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them they are one of the kindest people you know.
gc_a6_q2_r.hint: Say: You are one of the kindest people I know

gc_a6_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: You are genuinely one of the kindest people I have ever met. Let's keep going.

gc_a6_q3.say: Tell them their positive attitude brightens the room.
gc_a6_q3.hint: Say: Your positive attitude really brightens the room

gc_a6_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them their positive attitude brightens the room.
gc_a6_q3_r.hint: Say: Your positive attitude really brightens the room

gc_a6_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Your positive attitude genuinely brightens up the room for everyone. Let's keep going.

gc_a6_q4.say: Say that their kindness makes you want to be kinder too.
gc_a6_q4.hint: Say: Your kindness inspires me to be kinder too

gc_a6_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say that their kindness makes you want to be kinder too.
gc_a6_q4_r.hint: Say: Your kindness inspires me to be kinder too

gc_a6_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Your kindness truly inspires me to try to be a kinder person too. Let's keep going.

gc_a6_q5.say: End with: keep being exactly who you are.
gc_a6_q5.hint: Say: Keep being exactly who you are!

gc_a6_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. End with: keep being exactly who you are.
gc_a6_q5_r.hint: Say: Keep being exactly who you are!

gc_a6_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Keep being exactly who you are. The world needs more people like you. Let's keep going.

gc_a7_bridge.say: Complimenting a family member at home.

gc_a7_q1.say: At home, notice something kind your family member did.
gc_a7_q1.hint: Say: I notice you did something really kind today

gc_a7_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. At home, notice something kind your family member did.
gc_a7_q1_r.hint: Say: I notice you did something really kind today

gc_a7_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I noticed something really kind and thoughtful you did today. Let's keep going.

gc_a7_q2.say: Tell them you appreciate what they do for you.
gc_a7_q2.hint: Say: I appreciate everything you do for me

gc_a7_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them you appreciate what they do for you.
gc_a7_q2_r.hint: Say: I appreciate everything you do for me

gc_a7_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I appreciate everything you do for me. I don't say it enough. Let's keep going.

ar_a2_q3_r.hint: Say: Could you explain it one step at a time?

ar_a2_q4.say: They do. Tell them you are following better now.
ar_a2_q4.hint: Say: I am following much better now, thank you

ar_a2_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They do. Tell them you are following better now.
ar_a2_q4_r.hint: Say: I am following much better now, thank you

ar_a2_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am following much better now. Thank you for slowing down. Let's keep going.

ar_a2_q5.say: Ask if you can repeat back what you understood to check.
ar_a2_q5.hint: Say: Can I repeat back what I understood to check?

ar_a2_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask if you can repeat back what you understood to check.
ar_a2_q5_r.hint: Say: Can I repeat back what I understood to check?

ar_a2_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Can I repeat back what I understood just to make sure I got it right?. Let's keep going.

ar_a3_bridge.say: Asking a stranger to repeat something.

ar_a3_q1.say: A stranger says something to you on the street. You miss it.
ar_a3_q1.hint: Say: Sorry, I didn't quite hear you

ar_a3_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. A stranger says something to you on the street. You miss it.
ar_a3_q1_r.hint: Say: Sorry, I didn't quite hear you

ar_a3_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Sorry, I didn't quite hear what you said to me. Let's keep going.

ar_a3_q2.say: Politely ask them to repeat it.
ar_a3_q2.hint: Say: Could you please say that again?

ar_a3_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Politely ask them to repeat it.
ar_a3_q2_r.hint: Say: Could you please say that again?

ar_a3_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Could you please repeat what you just said? I am so sorry. Let's keep going.

ar_a3_q3.say: Lean in slightly and focus to hear better.
ar_a3_q3.hint: Say: I will focus carefully this time

ar_a3_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Lean in slightly and focus to hear better.
ar_a3_q3_r.hint: Say: I will focus carefully this time

ar_a3_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I will focus more carefully this time. Please go ahead. Let's keep going.

ar_a3_q4.say: They repeat it. Confirm you understood.
ar_a3_q4.hint: Say: I understand now. Thank you for repeating that

ar_a3_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They repeat it. Confirm you understood.
ar_a3_q4_r.hint: Say: I understand now. Thank you for repeating that

ar_a3_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I understand now. Thank you so much for repeating that for me. Let's keep going.

ar_a3_q5.say: Tell them thank you and have a nice day.
ar_a3_q5.hint: Say: Thank you! Have a really nice day!

ar_a3_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them thank you and have a nice day.
ar_a3_q5_r.hint: Say: Thank you! Have a really nice day!

ar_a3_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you so much! Have a really lovely day. Let's keep going.

ar_a4_q1.say: You are in a noisy place and someone is talking to you.
ar_a4_q1.hint: Say: It is quite loud here. Could you speak up?

ar_a4_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You are in a noisy place and someone is talking to you.
ar_a4_q1_r.hint: Say: It is quite loud here. Could you speak up?

ar_a4_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: It is quite loud here. Could you speak up a little please?. Let's keep going.

ar_a4_q2.hint: Say: Could we move somewhere quieter?

ar_a4_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask to move somewhere quieter.
ar_a4_q2_r.hint: Say: Could we move somewhere quieter?

ar_a4_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Could we maybe move somewhere quieter so I can hear you better?. Let's keep going.

ar_a4_q3.say: In the quieter spot, ask them to repeat what they said.
ar_a4_q3.hint: Say: Could you say that again now that it is quieter?

ar_a4_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. In the quieter spot, ask them to repeat what they said.
ar_a4_q3_r.hint: Say: Could you say that again now that it is quieter?

ar_a4_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Could you say that again now that it is a bit quieter here?. Let's keep going.

ar_a4_q4.say: They repeat it. Say thank you and that you can hear them clearly now.
ar_a4_q4.hint: Say: I can hear you clearly now. Thank you

ar_a4_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They repeat it. Say thank you and that you can hear them clearly now.
ar_a4_q4_r.hint: Say: I can hear you clearly now. Thank you

ar_a4_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I can hear you so much more clearly now. Thank you for moving with me. Let's keep going.

ar_a4_q5.hint: Say: Sorry for the inconvenience of moving. Thank you for understanding

ar_a4_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Apologise for any inconvenience caused.
ar_a4_q5_r.hint: Say: Sorry for the inconvenience of moving. Thank you for understanding

ar_a4_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Sorry for making us move. I really appreciate your understanding. Let's keep going.

ar_a5_bridge.say: Dealing with an accent or unfamiliar speech.

ar_a5_q1.say: Someone with an unfamiliar accent speaks to you. Stay respectful.
ar_a5_q1.hint: Say: Excuse me, could you please say that once more?

ar_a5_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Someone with an unfamiliar accent speaks to you. Stay respectful.
ar_a5_q1_r.hint: Say: Excuse me, could you please say that once more?

ar_a5_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me, could you please say that once more? I want to be sure. Let's keep going.

ar_a5_q2.say: Tell them you want to understand them properly.
ar_a5_q2.hint: Say: I want to make sure I understand you properly

ar_a5_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them you want to understand them properly.
ar_a5_q2_r.hint: Say: I want to make sure I understand you properly

ar_a5_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I want to make sure I understand you properly. Please do not mind. Let's keep going.

ar_a5_q3.say: Do not laugh or make them feel bad.
ar_a5_q3.hint: Say: I am listening carefully. Please continue

ar_a5_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Do not laugh or make them feel bad.
ar_a5_q3_r.hint: Say: I am listening carefully. Please continue

ar_a5_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am listening carefully and with full respect. Please continue. Let's keep going.

ar_a5_q4.say: They repeat and you understand. Thank them warmly.
ar_a5_q4.hint: Say: I understand now! Thank you so much

ar_a5_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They repeat and you understand. Thank them warmly.
ar_a5_q4_r.hint: Say: I understand now! Thank you so much

ar_a5_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I understand now! Thank you so much for your patience with me. Let's keep going.

ar_a5_q5.say: Tell them it was a pleasure talking to them.
ar_a5_q5.hint: Say: It was a pleasure talking to you!

ar_a5_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them it was a pleasure talking to them.
ar_a5_q5_r.hint: Say: It was a pleasure talking to you!

ar_a5_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: It was an absolute pleasure talking to you. Thank you. Let's keep going.

ar_a6_bridge.say: Asking a teacher to repeat in class.

ar_a6_q1.say: Your teacher says something important but you missed it.
ar_a6_q1.hint: Say: Excuse me, I missed what you just said

ar_a6_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Your teacher says something important but you missed it.
ar_a6_q1_r.hint: Say: Excuse me, I missed what you just said

ar_a6_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me, I missed what you just said. Could you repeat it?. Let's keep going.

ar_a6_q2.say: Raise your hand and politely ask the teacher to repeat.
ar_a6_q2.hint: Say: Could you please repeat that for me?

ar_a6_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Raise your hand and politely ask the teacher to repeat.
ar_a6_q2_r.hint: Say: Could you please repeat that for me?

ar_a6_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Could you please repeat that? I want to make sure I have the correct information. Let's keep going.

ar_a6_q3.say: The teacher repeats it. Say thank you.
ar_a6_q3.hint: Say: Thank you! I have it now

ar_a6_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The teacher repeats it. Say thank you.
ar_a6_q3_r.hint: Say: Thank you! I have it now

ar_a6_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you so much! I have it now. I appreciate you repeating it. Let's keep going.

ar_a6_q4.say: Write it down so you don't forget.
ar_a6_q4.hint: Say: I will write that down so I don't forget

ar_a6_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Write it down so you don't forget.
ar_a6_q4_r.hint: Say: I will write that down so I don't forget

ar_a6_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am going to write that down right now so I do not forget it. Let's keep going.

ar_a6_q5.say: Tell yourself it is always better to ask than to stay confused.
ar_a6_q5.hint: Say: It is always better to ask than to stay confused

ar_a6_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell yourself it is always better to ask than to stay confused.
ar_a6_q5_r.hint: Say: It is always better to ask than to stay confused

ar_a6_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: It is always better to ask a question than to stay confused and lost. Let's keep going.

ar_a7_q1.say: At a restaurant the waiter says your order and you miss it.
ar_a7_q1.hint: Say: Sorry, could you repeat my order?

ar_a7_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. At a restaurant the waiter says your order and you miss it.
ar_a7_q1_r.hint: Say: Sorry, could you repeat my order?

ar_a7_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Sorry, could you please repeat what I ordered? I did not hear clearly. Let's keep going.

ar_a7_q2.say: They repeat it. Confirm the order is correct.
ar_a7_q2.hint: Say: Yes that is correct! Thank you

ar_a7_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They repeat it. Confirm the order is correct.
ar_a7_q2_r.hint: Say: Yes that is correct! Thank you

ar_a7_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes, that is correct! Thank you for confirming that for me. Let's keep going.

ar_a7_q3.say: Ask if they could speak a little louder.
ar_a7_q3.hint: Say: Could you please speak a little louder?

ar_a7_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask if they could speak a little louder.
ar_a7_q3_r.hint: Say: Could you please speak a little louder?

ar_a7_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Could you please speak just a little louder? It is helpful. Let's keep going.

ar_a7_q4.say: Thank them for accommodating your request.
ar_a7_q4.hint: Say: Thank you for that! It is much easier to hear now

ar_a7_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Thank them for accommodating your request.
ar_a7_q4_r.hint: Say: Thank you for that! It is much easier to hear now

ar_a7_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you for that! It is so much easier to hear you now. Let's keep going.

ar_a7_q5.say: Leave the restaurant feeling proud that you spoke up.
ar_a7_q5.hint: Say: I am glad I spoke up! That was the right thing to do

ar_a7_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Leave the restaurant feeling proud that you spoke up.
ar_a7_q5_r.hint: Say: I am glad I spoke up! That was the right thing to do

ar_a7_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am glad I spoke up and asked. That was definitely the right thing to do. Let's keep going.

ar_a8_bridge.say: Final act — full confident ask to repeat.

ar_a8_q1.say: Someone says something very important. You only hear half of it.
ar_a8_q1.hint: Say: I only caught part of that. Could you say it again?

ar_a8_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Someone says something very important. You only hear half of it.
ar_a8_q1_r.hint: Say: I only caught part of that. Could you say it again?

ar_a8_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I only caught part of that. Could you please say the whole thing again?. Let's keep going.

ar_a8_q2.hint: Say: Could you please repeat that? I want to get it right

ar_a8_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask clearly and without embarrassment.
ar_a8_q2_r.hint: Say: Could you please repeat that? I want to get it right

ar_a8_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Could you please repeat that for me? I want to make sure I get it right. Let's keep going.

ar_a8_q3.hint: Say: I am listening very carefully now

ar_a8_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Listen intently this second time.
ar_a8_q3_r.hint: Say: I am listening very carefully now

ar_a8_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am listening very carefully this time. Please go ahead. Let's keep going.

ar_a8_q4.say: Confirm you now have the full information.
ar_a8_q4.hint: Say: I have the full picture now! Thank you

ar_a8_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Confirm you now have the full information.
ar_a8_q4_r.hint: Say: I have the full picture now! Thank you

ar_a8_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I have the full picture now. Thank you so much for repeating that. Let's keep going.

ar_a8_q5.say: Tell them you appreciate them never making you feel bad for asking.
ar_a8_q5.hint: Say: Thank you for never making me feel bad for asking

ar_a8_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them you appreciate them never making you feel bad for asking.
ar_a8_q5_r.hint: Say: Thank you for never making me feel bad for asking

ar_a8_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you for never making me feel bad about asking. I appreciate that. Let's keep going.

ar_win.say: Great job! Asking someone to repeat is a smart and brave thing to do. You earned a star!

so_a1_bridge.say: You have something to say in class discussion.

so_a1_q1.say: The teacher asks for opinions. Decide you want to share yours.
so_a1_q1.hint: Say: I want to share my opinion

so_a1_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The teacher asks for opinions. Decide you want to share yours.
so_a1_q1_r.hint: Say: I want to share my opinion

so_a1_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I have an opinion I would like to share if that is okay. Let's keep going.

so_a1_q2.say: Raise your hand to signal you want to speak.
so_a1_q2.hint: Say: I will raise my hand to show I want to speak

so_a1_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Raise your hand to signal you want to speak.
so_a1_q2_r.hint: Say: I will raise my hand to show I want to speak

so_a1_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I will raise my hand to let the teacher know I want to speak. Let's keep going.

so_a1_q3.say: The teacher calls on you. Take a breath before speaking.
so_a1_q3.hint: Say: Okay. Let me take a breath before I speak

so_a1_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The teacher calls on you. Take a breath before speaking.
so_a1_q3_r.hint: Say: Okay. Let me take a breath before I speak

so_a1_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Okay! Let me take a quick breath before I start speaking. Let's keep going.

so_a1_q4.say: Start your sentence with I think or In my opinion.
so_a1_q4.hint: Say: I think... or In my opinion...

so_a1_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Start your sentence with I think or In my opinion.
so_a1_q4_r.hint: Say: I think... or In my opinion...

so_a1_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: In my opinion, this is a really interesting topic because.... Let's keep going.

so_a1_q5.say: State your view clearly in 1-2 sentences.

so_a1_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. State your view clearly in 1-2 sentences.

so_a1_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I think that the character made the right choice because they were protecting their family. Let's keep going.

so_a2_bridge.say: Supporting your opinion with a reason.

so_a2_q1.say: Give one reason why you think what you think.
so_a2_q1.hint: Say: My reason for thinking this is...

so_a2_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Give one reason why you think what you think.
so_a2_q1_r.hint: Say: My reason for thinking this is...

so_a2_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: My reason for thinking this is that I have read something similar. Let's keep going.

so_a2_q2.say: Give an example from the text or lesson.
so_a2_q2.hint: Say: For example, in the text it says...

so_a2_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Give an example from the text or lesson.
so_a2_q2_r.hint: Say: For example, in the text it says...

so_a2_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: For example in the text it clearly says the character was afraid. Let's keep going.

so_a2_q3.say: Connect your example back to your opinion.
so_a2_q3.hint: Say: This shows that... which supports my view

so_a2_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Connect your example back to your opinion.
so_a2_q3_r.hint: Say: This shows that... which supports my view

so_a2_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: This shows that the character was scared, which supports my view. Let's keep going.

so_a2_q4.say: Speak clearly and at a good pace.
so_a2_q4.hint: Say: I will speak clearly and at a comfortable pace

so_a2_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Speak clearly and at a good pace.
so_a2_q4_r.hint: Say: I will speak clearly and at a comfortable pace

so_a2_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I will make sure to speak clearly and at a good pace for everyone. Let's keep going.

so_a2_q5.say: End your point with a clear conclusion.
so_a2_q5.hint: Say: So overall I believe that...

so_a2_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. End your point with a clear conclusion.
so_a2_q5_r.hint: Say: So overall I believe that...

so_a2_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: So overall I believe that the character did what they had to do. Let's keep going.

so_a3_q1.say: Another student shares a different view. Listen without interrupting.
so_a3_q1.hint: Say: I will listen without interrupting

so_a3_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Another student shares a different view. Listen without interrupting.
so_a3_q1_r.hint: Say: I will listen without interrupting

so_a3_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I will listen fully without interrupting until they are finished. Let's keep going.

so_a3_q2.say: Show you are listening by nodding or looking at the speaker.
so_a3_q2.hint: Say: I am showing that I am listening

so_a3_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Show you are listening by nodding or looking at the speaker.
so_a3_q2_r.hint: Say: I am showing that I am listening

so_a3_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am showing that I am paying full attention by looking at the speaker. Let's keep going.

so_a3_q3.say: When they finish, acknowledge their point.
so_a3_q3.hint: Say: I hear your point. That is interesting

so_a3_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. When they finish, acknowledge their point.
so_a3_q3_r.hint: Say: I hear your point. That is interesting

so_a3_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I hear your point. That is a really interesting way to look at it. Let's keep going.

so_a3_q4.say: Say you see it a bit differently.
so_a3_q4.hint: Say: I see it a little differently though

so_a3_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say you see it a bit differently.
so_a3_q4_r.hint: Say: I see it a little differently though

so_a3_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I see it a little differently though and here is why. Let's keep going.

so_a3_q5.say: Share your view again calmly and respectfully.
so_a3_q5.hint: Say: I still think that... because...

so_a3_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Share your view again calmly and respectfully.
so_a3_q5_r.hint: Say: I still think that... because...

so_a3_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I still think that the character was right because of what we read earlier. Let's keep going.

so_a4_bridge.say: Responding when someone disagrees with you.

so_a4_q1.say: A classmate says they disagree with you. Stay calm.
so_a4_q1.hint: Say: That is okay. We can see it differently

so_a4_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. A classmate says they disagree with you. Stay calm.
so_a4_q1_r.hint: Say: That is okay. We can see it differently

so_a4_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: That is completely okay. We can see it differently and that is fine. Let's keep going.

so_a4_q2.say: Ask them why they see it that way.
so_a4_q2.hint: Say: Why do you see it that way? I am curious

so_a4_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask them why they see it that way.
so_a4_q2_r.hint: Say: Why do you see it that way? I am curious

so_a4_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Why do you see it that way? I would genuinely like to understand. Let's keep going.

so_a4_q3.say: Listen to their reasoning with an open mind.
so_a4_q3.hint: Say: I am listening with an open mind

so_a4_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Listen to their reasoning with an open mind.
so_a4_q3_r.hint: Say: I am listening with an open mind

so_a4_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am listening with an open mind. Tell me your reasoning. Let's keep going.

so_a4_q4.say: Thank them for sharing their view.
so_a4_q4.hint: Say: Thank you for sharing your view

so_a4_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Thank them for sharing their view.
so_a4_q4_r.hint: Say: Thank you for sharing your view

so_a4_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you for sharing your view. I appreciate hearing it. Let's keep going.

so_a4_q5.say: Say you can both be right in different ways.
so_a4_q5.hint: Say: We can both be right in different ways

so_a4_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Say you can both be right in different ways.
so_a4_q5_r.hint: Say: We can both be right in different ways

so_a4_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: We can both be right in our own different ways. That is okay. Let's keep going.

so_a5_q1.say: You feel nervous before sharing. Remind yourself it is okay.
so_a5_q1.hint: Say: It is okay to be nervous. I can do this

so_a5_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You feel nervous before sharing. Remind yourself it is okay.
so_a5_q1_r.hint: Say: It is okay to be nervous. I can do this

so_a5_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: It is okay to feel nervous. I can do this. My voice matters. Let's keep going.

so_a5_q2.say: Tell yourself your opinion matters and deserves to be heard.
so_a5_q2.hint: Say: My opinion matters and I have the right to share it

so_a5_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell yourself your opinion matters and deserves to be heard.
so_a5_q2_r.hint: Say: My opinion matters and I have the right to share it

so_a5_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: My opinion matters and I absolutely have the right to share it. Let's keep going.

so_a5_q3.say: Sit up straight and prepare to speak.
so_a5_q3.hint: Say: I am going to sit up and get ready to speak

so_a5_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Sit up straight and prepare to speak.
so_a5_q3_r.hint: Say: I am going to sit up and get ready to speak

so_a5_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am going to sit up straight and get ready to share my view. Let's keep going.

so_a5_q4.hint: Say: I am raising my hand confidently

so_a5_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Raise your hand confidently.
so_a5_q4_r.hint: Say: I am raising my hand confidently

so_a5_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am raising my hand confidently to let the teacher know I am ready. Let's keep going.

so_a5_q5.say: Begin speaking with a strong clear voice.
so_a5_q5.hint: Say: I am going to speak clearly and confidently

so_a5_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Begin speaking with a strong clear voice.
so_a5_q5_r.hint: Say: I am going to speak clearly and confidently

so_a7_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They share and it is a great point. Tell them so.
so_a7_q3_r.hint: Say: That was a really great point! Well done for sharing

so_a7_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: That was a really great point! I am so glad you shared it. Let's keep going.

so_a7_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell them sharing opinions takes courage and they did it.

so_a8_q2_r.hint: Say: I am raising my hand straight away

so_win.say: Excellent! You shared your opinion clearly and confidently. That is a big deal. You earned a star!

jl_a5_q2_r.hint: Say: That is really interesting! I didn't know about that

jl_a6_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I have really enjoyed talking with you over lunch. Thank you. Let's keep going.

jl_a6_q2.say: Ask if they mind if you sit here again tomorrow.
jl_a6_q2.hint: Say: Do you mind if I sit here again tomorrow?

jl_a6_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ask if they mind if you sit here again tomorrow.
jl_a6_q2_r.hint: Say: Do you mind if I sit here again tomorrow?

jl_a6_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Do you mind if I come and sit here again tomorrow? I enjoyed this. Let's keep going.

jl_a6_q3_r.hint: Say: Great! I am really glad to hear that

jl_a7_q2_r.hint: Say: How has your morning been so far?

jl_a7_q3.say: They share something. Respond with interest.

jl_a7_q4_r.hint: Say: My morning was... I also had...

jl_a7_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: My morning was good. I also had a really interesting lesson in science. Let's keep going.

jl_a7_q5_r.hint: Say: I am really glad I decided to sit here yesterday

jl_a8_q3.say: Agree on a day and time.

jl_a8_q3_r.hint: Say: How about... day at... time?

jl_a8_q4_r.hint: Say: Should we exchange numbers so we can message?

jl_win.say: Wonderful! You sat with someone new and made a connection. You earned a star!

mw_a1_bridge.say: You realise your assignment is overdue.

mw_a1_q1_r.hint: Say: I need to be honest and tell my teacher

mw_a1_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I need to be honest with myself and tell my teacher the truth. Let's keep going.

mw_a1_q2_r.hint: Say: I am not going to hide it or make excuses

fr_a4_q2_r.hint: Say: I can feel frustrated but I choose how I act

fr_a4_q3_r.hint: Say: I will go for a short walk to clear my head

fr_a5_q3_r.hint: Say: Could you give me specific feedback on what I lost marks on?

fr_a6_q1_r.hint: Say: I am checking in with how I feel now

fr_a6_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am taking a moment to check in with how I am feeling now later in the day. Let's keep going.

fr_a6_q2.hint: Say: I notice that expressing my feelings really helped

fr_a6_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Notice that expressing your feelings helped.
fr_a6_q2_r.hint: Say: I notice that expressing my feelings really helped

fr_a6_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I notice that expressing and talking through my feelings really helped me today. Let's keep going.

fr_a6_q3.say: Tell yourself you handled the frustration well.
fr_a6_q3.hint: Say: I handled my frustration really well today

fr_a6_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Tell yourself you handled the frustration well.
fr_a6_q3_r.hint: Say: I handled my frustration really well today

fr_a6_q4.hint: Say: I am going to write in my journal about how I dealt with this

fr_a6_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Write in a journal how you dealt with it.
fr_a6_q4_r.hint: Say: I am going to write in my journal about how I dealt with this

fr_a6_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am going to write in my journal about how I managed my frustration today. Let's keep going.

fr_a6_q5.hint: Say: I will always express frustration calmly instead of acting out

fr_a6_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Commit to always using calm expression instead of acting out.

fr_a7_q1.hint: Say: I notice my friend looks frustrated

fr_a7_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You notice your friend seems very frustrated about something.
fr_a7_q1_r.hint: Say: I notice my friend looks frustrated

fr_a7_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I notice my friend looks really frustrated about something right now. Let's keep going.

fr_a7_q2.hint: Say: Are you okay? You seem frustrated

fr_a7_q2_r.hint: Say: Are you okay? You seem frustrated

fr_a7_q3.say: Listen to what they share without judgment.
fr_a7_q3.hint: Say: I am listening without any judgment

fr_a7_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Listen to what they share without judgment.
fr_a7_q3_r.hint: Say: I am listening without any judgment

fr_a7_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am listening to everything you say without any judgment whatsoever. Let's keep going.

fr_a7_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I completely hear you. That sounds genuinely really frustrating. I am sorry. Let's keep going.

fr_a7_q5_r.hint: Say: Would a short walk or a chat help?

fr_a7_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Would a short walk together or a good chat help you right now?. Let's keep going.

fr_a8_q1_r.hint: Say: I remember a time I managed my frustration really well

fr_win.say: Fantastic! You expressed your feelings calmly and clearly. That is really mature. You earned a star!

ab_a1_bridge.say: You start to feel overwhelmed during a lesson.
